info.title: turnstone Console API
info.description: Cluster-wide visibility and control across all turnstone nodes.

get.summary: Full cluster state snapshot
get.description: Returns the complete cluster state: all nodes with their workstreams and overview aggregates. Used for initial load and reconnection.

get.summary: Cluster SSE event stream
get.description: Server-Sent Events stream for real-time cluster updates. First event is a 'snapshot' with full cluster state, followed by node_joined, node_lost, cluster_state, ws_created, ws_closed, ws_rename events.

get.summary: Look up which node owns a workstream

post.summary: Create a new coordinator workstream
post.description: Allocates a console-hosted ``kind="coordinator"`` ChatSession.  200 on create; 429 when the ``coordinator.max_active`` cap is reached and no idle coordinator can be evicted.  Pre-1.5.0 this returned 201; the lifted ``create`` factory (Stage 2 verb lift) converges on 200 across both kinds.

get.summary: List coordinator workstreams visible to the caller
get.description: Returns coordinators owned by the caller.  Callers with ``admin.system`` see every coordinator across tenants.

get.summary: Get coordinator detail (rehydrates lazily on miss)
get.description: Returns the persisted coordinator's display fields.  If the session isn't currently in memory the manager rehydrates it before responding; ``500`` on rehydrate failure carries a correlation id matching the server log line.

post.summary: Open (rehydrate) a coordinator workstream by ws_id
post.description: Parity with ``POST /v1/api/workstreams/{ws_id}/open`` — gives SDK callers and operators a way to warm a coordinator without browsing to it.  Idempotent: ``already_loaded=true`` when the session was already in memory.

post.summary: Queue a user message onto the coordinator session
post.description: Worker thread picks up the message via the session's queue. Optional ``attachment_ids`` reserve attachments under the message's send_id token (parity with the interactive surface). Response carries ``attached_ids`` / ``dropped_attachment_ids`` so callers can detect partial reservations and ``priority`` / ``msg_id`` on the queued path. ``status: queue_full`` when the worker queue is full — caller should back off.

post.summary: Upload a file attachment to a coordinator workstream
post.description: Multipart upload (field ``file``). Same validation rules as the interactive surface: magic-byte image sniff, UTF-8 text decode, per-kind size cap, per-(ws,user) pending cap. Attachments stay pending until a subsequent ``/send`` reserves them under its ``send_id`` token.

get.summary: List the caller's pending coordinator attachments
Return raw bytes of a coordinator attachment
get.description: Same byte-stream + headers as the interactive surface. Text kinds are forced to ``text/plain`` so an HTML-shaped text upload can't render same-origin.

post.summary: Resolve a pending tool approval on the coordinator session
post.description: Approves or denies the pending tool call(s).  Set ``always`` to True to also add the pending tool name(s) to the session's auto-approve set so subsequent calls of the same tool skip the prompt.

post.summary: Cancel in-flight generation on the coordinator session
post.description: Drops the in-flight LLM call and unblocks any pending approval or plan review.  The coordinator state moves to idle; storage is preserved.

post.summary: Soft-close the coordinator (unload from memory; storage preserved)
post.description: Releases the worker thread + UI listeners and marks the row ``state=closed`` in storage.  The row remains queryable (audit / history) but cannot be reopened — a closed coordinator is terminal from the manager's perspective.

get.summary: Subscribe to the coordinator's SSE event stream
get.description: Server-Sent Events stream carrying ``status``, ``message``, ``tool_call``, ``tool_result``, ``approval``, ``error``, and the phase-3 ``child_ws_*`` fan-out events.  Pings every 5s.  Body is text/event-stream — the response schema is omitted from the catalog because OpenAPI 3.1 has no first-class SSE type.

get.summary: Read the coordinator's reconstructed message history
get.description: Returns the tail of the conversation in OpenAI-like message format.  Used by the page-load handshake; SSE handles updates after that.  Bounded by the ``limit`` query parameter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max conversation rows to fetch from storage (default 100, max 500).

get.summary: List the coordinator's spawned child workstreams
get.description: Returns interactive child workstreams whose ``parent_ws_id`` is this coordinator.  Same row shape as the model-facing ``list_workstreams`` tool so the tree UI and the tool agree.

get.summary: Read the coordinator's task list envelope
get.description: Returns the ``{version, tasks}`` envelope persisted via the ``tasks`` model tool.  Corrupt envelopes return an empty list (the tool itself surfaces corruption errors on mutation).

post.summary: Toggle trusted-session mode for send_to_workstream
post.description: When enabled, ``send_to_workstream`` calls that target a ws_id in the coordinator's own subtree skip the approval prompt.  Foreign ws_ids continue to require approval — trust only relaxes the guard for work the orchestrator itself spawned.  Every auto-approved send still emits a ``coordinator.send.auto_approved`` audit row so the trail isn't lost.  Gated on both ``admin.coordinator`` AND ``coordinator.trust.send`` so the trust feature is an explicit opt-in capability separate from ordinary coordinator administration.

post.summary: Revoke tool access on a live coordinator session
post.description: Adds the named tools to the coordinator session's revoked set without closing the session.  The model can keep working on whatever is already in flight but cannot invoke the revoked tools again.  Idempotent and additive — calling twice with disjoint lists unions them.  Revocations do not survive a session close / reopen; operators opt in per session.  Writes ``coordinator.restricted`` with the revocation delta and the full post-state.

post.summary: Cancel the coordinator and every direct child
post.description: Cancels the coordinator's in-flight generation AND dispatches ``cancel_workstream`` through the routing proxy for every direct child in the in-memory registry.  Grandchildren are not touched directly — they sit behind their parent's cancel, which propagates via the child's SSE stream.  Returns the per-child disposition (``cancelled`` / ``failed``) so the UI can show which children responded.  Writes ``coordinator.stopped_cascade`` with the two lists.

post.summary: Soft-close every direct child of the coordinator
post.description: Reads the in-memory child registry and dispatches ``close_workstream`` via the routing proxy for every direct child under a bounded (16-concurrency) semaphore.  Unlike ``stop_cascade`` this does not touch grandchildren — the model-facing tool asks for a bounded teardown of its own fan-out.  Returns ``{closed, failed, skipped}`` where ``skipped`` distinguishes already-gone (404) from dispatch-broken (``failed``).  The optional ``reason`` propagates to every closed child's audit + workstream_config.  Writes ``coordinator.closed_all_children`` at the coord level.

get.summary: Cluster-wide live workstream detail (storage + live block + tail)
get.description: Aggregates the persisted row, a best-effort live block from the owning node (or the in-process coordinator manager for ``kind="coordinator"`` rows), and the tail of the message history.  Gated on the ``admin.cluster.inspect`` permission (granted to ``builtin-admin`` via migration 040; revoke or reassign to a custom role for tighter control).  ``live`` is null on node unreachability / 5xx so callers can degrade gracefully.

parameters.name: message_limit
parameters.description: Max conversation rows in the tail (default 20, clamped to 200).

get.summary: Console health check

key.description: Dotted setting key that was reset
key.title: Key

default.description: Registry default value the setting reverted to
default.title: Default

AuthLoginRequest.description:
POST /v1/api/auth/login request body.

Either username+password or token must be provided.
AuthLoginRequest.title: AuthLoginRequest

jwt.description: JWT session token (if JWT auth is configured)
jwt.title: Jwt

token.description: Raw API token — save this, it cannot be retrieved again
token.title: Token

TokenInfo.description: Token metadata (never includes the hash or raw token).
TokenInfo.title: TokenInfo

ClusterWsDetailResponse.description:
Response body for GET /v1/api/cluster/ws/{ws_id}/detail.

Aggregates the persisted workstream row with a best-effort live block
fetched from the owning node (or the in-process coordinator manager
for ``kind="coordinator"`` rows).  ``live`` is ``null`` when the
owning node is unreachable, returns 5xx, or doesn't have the row in
its in-memory dashboard cache — callers degrade gracefully without
treating it as an error.

persisted.description: Full storage row for the workstream (state, kind, parent_ws_id, etc.).
persisted.title: Persisted

live.description: Live in-flight counters (state, tokens, activity, pending_approval) when the owning node returns them; null on degrade.
live.title: Live

messages.description: Tail of the workstream's reconstructed message history.
messages.title: Messages

ClusterWsDetailResponse.title: ClusterWsDetailResponse

node_id.description: Target node: specific ID, 'auto', 'pool', or empty for auto
node_id.title: Node Id

initial_message.description: Optional first message sent after creation
initial_message.title: Initial Message

resume_ws.description: Workstream ID to resume (loads previous conversation)
resume_ws.title: Resume Ws

judge_model.description: Override judge model alias for this workstream
judge_model.title: Judge Model

approved.description: True approves the pending tool call(s); False denies.
approved.title: Approved

feedback.description: Optional human feedback string forwarded to the model.
feedback.title: Feedback

always.description: When approved=True, also adds the pending tool name(s) to the session's auto-approve set so subsequent calls of the same tool skip the prompt.
always.title: Always

CoordinatorChildInfo.description: Per-row shape in the coordinator children listing.
CoordinatorChildInfo.title: CoordinatorChildInfo

truncated.description: True when the storage page filled the cap; more rows may exist.
truncated.title: Truncated

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason — propagated to every closed child's audit + workstream_config for postmortem.  Capped at 512 chars; the handler returns 400 on overflow.
reason.title: Reason

closed.description: Child ws_ids that accepted the close dispatch.
closed.title: Closed

failed.description: Child ws_ids whose close dispatch returned an error other than an already-gone 404 — the cascade continues on per-child failure so a single unreachable node doesn't abort the whole batch.
failed.title: Failed

skipped.description: Child ws_ids that returned 404 on close (already gone).  Reported separately from ``failed`` so operators can distinguish already-done from dispatch-broken.
skipped.title: Skipped

name.description: Optional display name; auto-generated when empty.
name.title: Name

skill.description: Optional skill name to apply to the coordinator session.
skill.title: Skill

initial_message.description: Optional first user message dispatched to the new coordinator session.
initial_message.title: Initial Message

already_loaded.description: True when the coordinator was already in memory; absent after a fresh rehydrate.
already_loaded.title: Already Loaded

revoke.description: Tool names to add to the session's revoked set.  Once revoked the coordinator cannot invoke the named tools on subsequent turns without closing and re-opening the session.
revoke.title: Revoke

revoked_tools.description: Full post-revocation set of revoked tool names.
revoked_tools.title: Revoked Tools

message.description: User message to queue onto the coordinator's worker.
message.title: Message

attachment_ids.description: Explicit list of attachment ids to inject into this turn. When omitted, any pending attachments for the caller on this coordinator are auto-consumed. An empty list disables auto-consumption for this send.
attachment_ids.title: Attachment Ids

attached_ids.description: Attachment ids actually reserved onto this turn. Subset of the request's `attachment_ids` (or the auto-consumed pending set). Empty when the send carries no attachments.
attached_ids.title: Attached Ids

dropped_attachment_ids.description: Attachment ids the caller requested that the server could not reserve (lost a race, already consumed, or cross-scope). The request still proceeds with whatever was reserved.
dropped_attachment_ids.title: Dropped Attachment Ids

priority.description: Set on `queued` responses: relative priority of the queued message.
priority.title: Priority

msg_id.description: Set on `queued` responses: id used to dequeue the message.
msg_id.title: Msg Id

cancelled.description: Child ws_ids that accepted the cancel dispatch.
cancelled.title: Cancelled

failed.description: Child ws_ids whose cancel dispatch returned an error other than an already-gone 404 — the cascade continues on per-child failure so a single unreachable node doesn't abort the whole batch.
failed.title: Failed

skipped.description: Child ws_ids that returned 404 on cancel (already gone).  Reported separately from ``failed`` so operators can distinguish already-done from dispatch-broken.
skipped.title: Skipped

CoordinatorTaskInfo.description: Per-task row in the coordinator's task envelope.

status.description: One of: pending / in_progress / done / blocked.
status.title: Status

CoordinatorTaskInfo.title: CoordinatorTaskInfo

CoordinatorTasksResponse.description:
Response body for GET /v1/api/workstreams/{ws_id}/tasks.

Mirrors the envelope the ``tasks(action='list')`` model tool returns.
CoordinatorTasksResponse.title: CoordinatorTasksResponse

send.description: When true, ``send_to_workstream`` calls that target a ws_id in the coordinator's own subtree skip the approval prompt.  Foreign ws_ids continue to require approval — trust only relaxes the guard for work the orchestrator itself spawned.
send.title: Send

target_mode.description: auto, pool, all, or specific node_id
target_mode.title: Target Mode

initial_message.description: Message sent to the new workstream
initial_message.title: Initial Message

ScheduleRunInfo.description: Single execution record for a scheduled task.
ScheduleRunInfo.title: ScheduleRunInfo

config.description: JSON config object with mcpServers key
config.title: Config

SkillKind.description:
Which ``list_skills`` surface a skill row is visible on.

``INTERACTIVE`` — only the interactive-session activation path sees
this row.  ``COORDINATOR`` — only the coordinator's ``list_skills``
tool sees this row.  ``ANY`` — both sides (default for legacy rows
predating the classifier).
SkillKind.title: SkillKind

description.description: Human-readable description surfaced by ``list_skills`` and the admin UI.  Must be non-empty — catches skills registered without thinking about discoverability before they reach a model's tool-selection prompt.
description.title: Description

kind.description: Classifier routing the skill to ``list_skills`` calls.  ``interactive`` is visible only to the interactive-session activation path; ``coordinator`` is visible only to the coordinator's ``list_skills`` tool; ``any`` (default) is visible on both sides, which preserves pre-upgrade behaviour for legacy rows.

description.description: When present, replaces the skill description.  Must be non-empty — the admin endpoint rejects a blanking update.
description.title: Description

kind.description: When present, updates the skill's classifier.  Same accepted values as ``CreateSkillRequest.kind``.

RouteResponse.description: Route lookup result — which node owns a workstream.
RouteResponse.title: RouteResponse

RouteCreateResponse.description: Workstream creation via the routing proxy.
RouteCreateResponse.title: RouteCreateResponse

WorkstreamDetailResponse.description:
Response body for ``GET /v1/api/workstreams/{ws_id}``.

Renamed and relocated from ``CoordinatorDetailResponse`` in the
Stage 2 history/detail verb lift. Both kinds populate every field;
SDK consumers don't branch on kind to read them. The lift adds the
endpoint to interactive as a feature gain (pre-lift only coord
exposed it).
WorkstreamDetailResponse.title: WorkstreamDetailResponse

WorkstreamKind.description:
Classifier for which manager hosts a workstream.

StrEnum so members are drop-in ``str`` replacements for the DB column,
JSON payloads, and existing ``==`` comparisons against raw strings.
Narrow internal annotations to this type; wide boundaries (HTTP body,
DB row) stay ``str`` and parse via ``WorkstreamKind(raw)`` / ``from_raw``
at the edge.
WorkstreamKind.title: WorkstreamKind

WorkstreamHistoryResponse.description:
Response body for ``GET /v1/api/workstreams/{ws_id}/history``.

Renamed and relocated from ``CoordinatorHistoryResponse`` in the
Stage 2 history/detail verb lift. Same OpenAI-like message-row
shape on both kinds; the lift adds the endpoint to interactive as
a feature gain (pre-lift interactive only exposed history through
the SSE replay on ``/events``).

messages.description: Tail of the workstream's reconstructed message history (provider-fidelity OpenAI-like shape). Bounded by the ``limit`` query parameter (default 100, max 500).
messages.title: Messages

WorkstreamHistoryResponse.title: WorkstreamHistoryResponse
